subject: [POLICY-22] Fix issue to pass Jenkins build
commit_message:
[POLICY-22] Fix issue to pass Jenkins build

Change-Id: Icfa18ee277b6c99bb442612227a47db572a74586
Signed-off-by: Gao, Chenfei (cg287m) <chenfei.gao11@gmail.com>

entries.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

Patch Set 1:

Patch Set 1:

Chenfei, these "autogenerated_*.xml", are they built during the test process? Would they change every time? If so, then we should not have them actually residing in gerrit. Instead we would create a .gitignore entry so people don't accidentally store them in their when doing the commit process.

Patch Set 1:

> Chenfei, these "autogenerated_*.xml", are they built during the
 > test process? Would they change every time? If so, then we should
 > not have them actually residing in gerrit. Instead we would create
 > a .gitignore entry so people don't accidentally store them in their
 > when doing the commit process.

 > Chenfei, these "autogenerated_*.xml", are they built during the
 > test process? Would they change every time? If so, then we should
 > not have them actually residing in gerrit. Instead we would create
 > a .gitignore entry so people don't accidentally store them in their
 > when doing the commit process.

These "autogenerated_*.xml" are generated during the test process. They will change every time. They should not be in gerrit commit. I will put them in .gitignore and submit a patch.
Patch Set 1: Code-Review+2

Patch Set 1:

Because of the renaming of onap in drools-apps, we need counterpart changes in the docker side to pull in the correct apps zip.   

Can you submit counterpart changes in docker, the root pom.xml, search for maven-dependency-plugin, and change coordinates for the apps zip package.  I think that's the only change that is needed there in order to have their installation automated.

Let's try to submit both patches at the same time to avoid integration problems when using the staging version, as there are integration testing activities going on with all components.

Other than that, looks good, I'll approve once I see the docker review with that change.

Patch Set 1: -Code-Review

Chenfei, use POLICY-53 as the JIRA issue to do the docker script changes mentioned by Jorge.

Patch Set 1:

Chenfie, acturally there is a bit more changes that need to be done in policy/engine side as side effects of the openecomp to onap changes.   We would need to get Ravi involved, can have email conversation offline.  

grep on policy/engine repo:

policy/engine/BRMSGateway/dependency.json
policy/engine/BRMSGateway/src/main/java/org/openecomp/policy/brmsInterface/BRMSPush.java
policy/engine/ECOMP-SDK-APP/src/main/webapp/static/fusion/images/ecomp.png
policy/engine/packages/base/src/files/install/mysql/data/161000_upgrade_script.sql
policy/engine/packages/base/src/files/install/servers/brmsgw/dependency.json
policy/engine/packages/base/src/files/install/servers/pap/webapps/Config/com.Config_BRMS_Param_BRMSParamvFWDemoPolicy.1.txt
policy/engine/packages/base/src/files/install/servers/pap/webapps/Config/com.Config_BRMS_Param_BRMSParamvLBDemoPolicy.1.txt
policy/engine/PolicyEngineClient/testResources/test.drl

We probably need to have a discussion with pe side to see if can streamline this process a little bit, to reduce work on the PE side everytime there are new dependencies, template changes, etc ..

Patch Set 1:

Yes we would need these changes also. Keep in mind we have JIRA Epics/US's in place to streamline the whole template development process going forward. But we have to maintain backward compatibility until that architecture is in place.

Patch Set 1:

Jorge, let's schedule a working session with Ravi together in the coming week. I need some guidance on the changes to docker script.

Patch Set 1:

Chenfei, please abandon this code inspection and 5593.   In summary, abandon:

https://gerrit.onap.org/r/#/c/5579
https://gerrit.onap.org/r/#/c/5593

Create a new inspection from the latest master version and update the headers in the following files to conform to ONAP licensing:

controlloop/templates/template.demo/src/main/resources/ControlLoop_Template_xacml_guard.drl
controlloop/templates/template.demo/src/main/resources/ControlLoop_Template_1707_xacml_guard.drl
controlloop/templates/template.demo/src/test/java/org/onap/policy/controlloop/processor/ControlLoopXacmlGuardTest.java
controlloop/templates/template.demo/src/test/java/org/onap/policy/controlloop/processor/Util.java

Also add the .gitignore from 5593 to ignore autogenerated files in the new inspection package (see https://gerrit.onap.org/r/#/c/5593).

Then, I believe we'll be good.

Patch Set 1:

Yes we should abandon since we patched the original with these changes.
Abandoned
